content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Inspected the prompt-contract generator, workflow-stage consumers, template updates, CLI wiring, and related tests. I found one substantive regression: the global prompt-hash algorithm changed without backward compatibility, so unchanged legacy prompts will be misclassified as drifted during resume.

## Amendments

1. Compatibility regression: `src/adapters/fs.rs:249-253` changes `FileSystem::prompt_hash` to SHA-256, but `src/contexts/workflow_composition/drift.rs:62-90` still compares the freshly computed hash to the persisted `prompt_hash_at_cycle_start` with exact equality. Any interrupted or paused run created before this change will now look like prompt drift even when `prompt.md` is unchanged, causing bogus continue/restart/abort behavior on resume. Preserve the previous persisted hash format or add an explicit migration/dual-hash compatibility path for legacy snapshots/project records.
   Rationale: This breaks resume correctness for existing projects and changes behavior well outside the prompt-contract scope.
